title: qPCR and RT-PCR
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"14rc","text":"The protocol describes the methodology and materials we use in the Di Monte lab for mRNA extraction and q-PCR/or RT-PCR analyses from fixed tissue samples. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Dissect the dorsal medulla oblongata from paraformaldehyde-fixed coronal sections of the medulla oblongata.
Extract total RNA using Nucleic Acid Isolation Kit (Ambion), and measure yield using a nanodrop.
Mix the PCR products with 6x sample buffer (New England Biolabs) and 5% DMSO and load on 2.0% SeaKem agarose gel (Lonza Bioscience) pre-stained with RedSafe dye (1:20,000, Intron Biotechnology)
Synthesize cDNA from 100 ng template RNA using SuperScriptVILO Master Mix (Life Technologies) and the suitable primers ( 20 µL final volume).
Run the PCR with a real-time PCR machine (e.g., StepOnePlusTM real-time PCR system, Applied Biosystems) in triplicates.
Prepare a 20 µL l reaction mix containing 1 m l cDNA, Power SYBRGreen (Applied Biosystems) and primers.
If necessary, extract RNA from human brain (Agilent Technologies) as a reference sample.
creator: Ayse Ulusoy
